mnemonics.title: Noise Effects Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: HEAR: Hearing loss, Endocrine stress, Cardiovascular issues, Reduced sleep - remembering Health Effects And Risks
S-I-L-E-N-T for Noise Control: Substitution, Isolation, Legislative, Engineering, Noise barriers, Treatment/PPE
mnemonics.title: Noise Control Measures

acronyms.usage: Measuring noise levels, e.g., 85 dB threshold for hearing damage

acronyms.expansion: A-weighted decibel - accounts for human hearing frequency response
acronyms.usage: Environmental noise standards and occupational exposure assessment
Calculating average noise exposure over time periods
Earplugs, earmuffs for worker noise protection
Setting workplace noise exposure limits and regulations

visualTips.visualization: Picture a ruler from 0 (silence) to 180 (jet takeoff). 0=whisper, 60=normal conversation, 85=heavy traffic, 120=rock concert, 180=rocket launch. Remember: every 10 dB = 10x intensity
Imagine ripples in water from a stone - sound travels as expanding pressure waves outward from source, getting weaker with distance
Earplugs block ~20-25 dB, earmuffs block ~25-30 dB, together they can block 35+ dB - visualize them as sound-absorbing walls at your ears

analogies.analogy: Like earthquake Richter scale - each unit represents a large jump in actual energy, not just a small increase
Like bending grass blades - occasional bending recovers, but constant trampling flattens permanently. Hair cells in ear work similarly - overwork leads to permanent damage
Like a umbrella blocking rain - barriers block sound waves from reaching you, though some sound 'leaks' around edges
